description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.           In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.           For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.          **A few examples of your responsibilities**  * **Frame the right questions.**   Partner with product and growth manager to identify the most impactful questions—whether about pricing, feature adoption, or user behavior—and define how to answer them * **Own product analytics and experiments.**   Design, implement, and analyze experiments end to end (e.g., deciding whether a feature should be paid or free), balancing short\-term impact with long\-term product health. * **Translate data into insights.**   Provide timely, high\-quality insights to support prioritization, business planning, and product trade\-offs. * **Make insights actionable.**   Translate analyses and model outputs into recommendations, using dashboards and lightweight reporting to help teams move quickly. * **Leverage AI to accelerate work and uncover insights.**   Use AI tools to move faster, surface ideas and opportunities, and study competing products and trends.   **What we look for**  * **Reasoning ability.**   Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.**   You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.**   You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.**   You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.   **What we offer**  * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.**   You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.**   We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.**   Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.**   Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.   **Commitment \& contract**  Permanent or fixed\-term. Full\-time or part\-time.          **Location**  Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.          **The selection process**  In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.           If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.           We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.           We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.           Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.           To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.           If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.           In this three\-month summer internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth.          **A few examples of your responsibilities**  * **Understand.**   Build deep knowledge of your product, its business model, and the key levers driving growth. * **Analyze.**   Use AI, automation, and advanced analytics to explore high\-performing campaigns, identify patterns in user and product data, and generate actionable insights. * **Ideate.**   Turn insights, experiment results, competitor research, and AI\-assisted exploration into hypotheses and identify opportunities to improve growth and monetization. * **Test.**   Design and run rigorous experiments with real users, partnering closely with designers, engineers, and analysts to bring ideas to life and iterate quickly based on results. * **Scale.**   Roll out winning ideas broadly, and use learnings from each experiment to shape the next cycle of discovery and testing.   **What we look for**  * **Reasoning ability.**   Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.**   You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.**   You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.**   You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.   **What we offer**  * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.**   You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.**   We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.**   Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.**   Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.   **Commitment \& contract**  Internship. Full\-time or part\-time.          **Location**  Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.          **The selection process**  In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.           If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.           We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.           We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.           Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.           To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.           If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

Work closely with:     * Sales /Sales Engineering * Network Operations / Customer Outcome * Marketing * Exchange Compliance \& Reporting * Legal \& Compliance            **How You Will Make an Impact:**     * **Show Your Enthusiasm:**   Your motivation and eagerness to learn will bring positive energy to our team. * **Take Initiative:**   Don’t be afraid to propose new ideas and solutions. learning opportunity to develop your skills and knowledge. * **Adapt and Innovate:**   Be open to new ways of working and embrace changes that can lead to improvements.            **Essential Skills and Experience to be Successful in the Role:**     * Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in progress. * Advanced English language skills. * Understanding or strong interest in financial industry technologies. * Strong communication and organization skills (leading small meetings, driving objectives, tracking initiatives). * Demonstrated ability to leverage AI tools to support research, content development, analysis, or process improvement. * Exposure to using AI applications and data management techniques to improve information accuracy and workflow efficiency is desirable. * Previous experience supporting a product introduction or market launch is a plus. * Strong analytical skills and ability to work in fast\-paced and multi\-disciplinary environments.            **Curious about what we offer?**     * 9 weeks full\-time paid internship filled with exciting events and initiatives * Access to LinkedIn Learning * Access to IPC University * Improve your technical and soft skills through integrated training and development workshops * Gain invaluable exposure to leadership and the corporate environment * Build lasting connections through networking and relationship\-building opportunities * Connect with your peers through exciting social events            **Additional Information:**       At IPC, we believe that hybrid working creates an inclusive, flexible environment where employees can perform at their best, and teams can collaborate, innovate, and celebrate successes together. Some individuals may be required to work from the office or client sites more than 60% of the time, if required by their role and/or client needs. We live and breathe our commitment to innovation by embracing bold ideas, seizing new opportunities and striving for excellence. Our people have continued to deliver ground\-breaking solutions to our clients for over 50 years.

We have been asked to asked by our client to run a campaign to identify entry level interns for this summer 2026\. Our client is a bulge bracket bank and one of the pre\-eminent investment banks. We will run campaigns for them on an ongoing basis through the first half of this year.               Our client is looking for people in their global offices and we will post these roles for each location. Please apply to the location closest to you.               You will either be placed in their FIG or TMT Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry people also in energy, Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.               The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity\-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.                      **Your Focus**       As an Investment Banking Analyst \- Intern, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.               The role is execution\-driven and detail\-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.               This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high\-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.                      **Responsibilities**     * Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M\&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates * Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options * Prepare client\-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation * Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution * Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines * Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders * Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials * Assess transaction\-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements * Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work * Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams             This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.               You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well\-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.                      **Minimum Qualifications**      **Graduate – Analyst**     * At university studying any degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based including Scientific, Engineering, Arts etc * Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client\-ready materials * High attention to detail and strong organisational skills * Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities * Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations                    **Process**       This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. description: **About the Company**                        Apollo Research    is a rapidly growing AI safety and model evaluation company, providing third\-party evaluations for frontier AI models. The capabilities of current AI systems are evolving rapidly. While these advancements offer tremendous opportunities, they also present significant risks, such as the potential for deliberate misuse or the deployment of sophisticated yet misaligned models. At Apollo Research, our primary concern lies with deceptive alignment, a phenomenon where a model appears aligned but is, in fact, misaligned and capable of evading human oversight.               At Apollo, we aim for a culture that emphasizes truth\-seeking, being goal\-oriented, giving and receiving constructive feedback, and being friendly and helpful. If you’re interested in more details about what it’s like working at Apollo, you can find more information    here    .              **About the Role**                       You’ll be Apollo’s second Finance hire. You’ll work with our Senior Finance Manager to execute the day\-to\-day engine that keeps Apollo running and convert business activity into consistent, trusted numbers for management and investors. This role starts part\-time, about 1\.5 to 2 days a week, in person in London. As we double in size over the next 12 months and you show your strengths, your responsibilities and days will increase, with a clear path to a full\-time position. This isn’t a side gig; we’re looking for someone who wants to grow with us. You'd be able to help build a finance team from the ground up.                      **Responsibilities**                     * Run the Procure\-to\-Pay and Order\-to\-Cash cycles * Handle supplier invoices and receipts * Create customer invoices * Prepare supplier payments * Follow up on overdue balances * Ensure every transaction is recorded accurately in Xero * Reconcile bank accounts across our multi\-entity and multi\-currency operations to keep our cash positions accurate every week                **Qualifications**                     * You’re at least part\-qualified (ACA, CIMA, ACCA, AAT) * You understand double\-entry bookkeeping intuitively and can visualize how a transaction flows through the ledger without guidance * You have 2\+ years experience in a transactional processing role managing high\-volume Order\-to\-Cash and Procure\-to\-Pay cycles without letting quality slip                **Required Skills**                     * You’re a wizard with Xero and modern finance platforms like Payhawk, Revolut, Mercury, or similar tools * You use AI tools daily to work faster and smarter * You can pick up new AI tools quickly and know when to use them and, just as importantly, when not to * You are meticulous and spot variances and mistakes * You act with integrity and meet deadlines * You are high\-agency and take initiative to solve problems * You are hungry to develop and want this role to become full\-time                **Preferred Skills**                     * Experience in a fast\-paced startup environment * Strong analytical skills * Ability to work collaboratively in a team                **Pay range and compensation package**                       £220–280 per day as a contractor at first, moving to a pro\-rata salary based on £40–50k FTE in the first 3\-6 months.                      **Equal Opportunity Statement**                       Apollo Research is an Equal Opportunity Employer. We value diversity and are committed to providing equal opportunities to all, regardless of age, disability, gender reassignment, marriage and civil partnership, pregnancy and maternity, race, religion or belief, sex, or sexual orientation. Your Privacy and Fairness in Our Recruitment Process: We are committed to protecting your data, ensuring fairness, and adhering to workplace fairness principles in our recruitment process. To enhance hiring efficiency, we use AI\-powered tools to assist with tasks such as resume screening. These tools are designed and deployed in compliance with internationally recognized AI governance frameworks. Your personal data is handled securely and transparently. We adopt a human\-centred approach: all resumes are screened by a human and final hiring decisions are made by our team. If you have questions about how your data is processed or wish to report concerns about fairness, please contact us at info@apolloresearch.ai.                       Applications deadline: We encourage early submissions and are aiming for a 1 May 2026 start date.

description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.           In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.           For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.           In this three\-month summer internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth.          **A few examples of your responsibilities**  * **Understand.**   Build deep knowledge of your product, its business model, and the key levers driving growth. * **Analyze.**   Use AI, automation, and advanced analytics to explore high\-performing campaigns, identify patterns in user and product data, and generate actionable insights. * **Ideate.**   Turn insights, experiment results, competitor research, and AI\-assisted exploration into hypotheses and identify opportunities to improve growth and monetization. * **Test.**   Design and run rigorous experiments with real users, partnering closely with designers, engineers, and analysts to bring ideas to life and iterate quickly based on results. * **Scale.**   Roll out winning ideas broadly, and use learnings from each experiment to shape the next cycle of discovery and testing.   **What we look for**  * **Reasoning ability.**   Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.**   You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.**   You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.**   You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.   **What we offer**  * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.**   You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.**   We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.**   Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.**   Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.   **Commitment \& contract**  Internship. Full\-time or part\-time.          **Location**  Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.          **The selection process**  In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.           If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.           We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.           We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.           Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.           To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.           If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

description: **Driving reliable global payroll through precision, partnership, and scalable systems.**               Work shouldn't just be something we do; it should have a purpose. This role will be based in the UK and will report to the Global Payroll Manager (based in the US). The GPC will coordinate payroll activities for employees in the US, UK, Hungary, Serbia, Portugal, Canada, Japan, Australia and the Netherlands.               The GPC will work closely with internal stakeholders, locally and internationally based payroll providers, and HR to ensure payroll is processed accurately, on time, and in compliance with regulatory guidelines. Experience with  **ADP Workforce Now (“WFN”)**   is essential, particularly for US payroll operations. The GPC will also be a key participant in systems integrations as Envision aligns workflows to help scale our growing / international business.              **How will you make an impact at Envision Pharma Group?**               Role responsibilities     * Coordinate end\-to\-end payroll processing across multiple countries, ensuring accuracy, timeliness, and compliance. * Act as the primary payroll contact for assigned regions, including the US, UK, Hungary, Serbia, Portugal, Canada, Japan, Australia, and the Netherlands. * Manage monthly, bi\-weekly, and ad\-hoc payroll cycles in partnership with ADP and local/international payroll vendors. * Administer payroll activities using ADP WFN, including data validation, payroll processing, reconciliations, and reporting. * Ensure payroll inputs (new hires, terminations, salary changes, bonuses, commissions, benefits, deductions, and timecards) are accurately processed and supported by appropriate source documentation. * Partner with ADP and third\-party payroll providers to resolve payroll queries, discrepancies, and system issues. * Ensure compliance with local payroll legislation, tax requirements, and statutory reporting obligations in all supported countries. * Maintain strong payroll controls, including reconciliations, approvals, audit trails, and checklists; collaborate with HR and other stakeholders to ensure high\-quality data entry into the HRIS. * Support internal and external audits, payroll reconciliations, and year\-end activities (e.g., W\-2s, P60s, P11Ds, T4s), including reconciliation of global payroll to the general ledger. * Ensure timely submission and approval of US non\-exempt employee timecards for bi\-weekly payroll processing. * Collaborate closely with HR, Finance, Benefits, and external vendors to ensure accurate payroll outcomes. * Respond to employee payroll inquiries in a professional and timely manner (within 48 business hours). * Provide payroll\-related guidance to HR, including tax treatment, benefits, and off\-cycle payments. * Produce payroll reports for Finance, including journals and variance analysis. * Identify and implement opportunities to improve payroll processes, standardisation, and automation. * Support payroll transitions, implementations, and system enhancements as required.            **Do you have what we are looking for?**               Skills \& expertise     * Payroll, HR, or Finance qualification (e.g., CIPP, CPP) preferred, but not required. * Proven experience coordinating multi\-country payroll in a corporate environment. * Strong hands\-on experience with ADP Workforce Now (“WFN”). * Solid understanding of UK and US payroll processes, with exposure to additional international payrolls. * Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple payroll calendars. * Excellent organisational and time\-management skills. * Strong communication skills with the ability to work across multiple time zones (primarily US and UK).            **\#TogetherWeDiscover**               Do you have the experience we are looking for? If so, explore your place at Envision today!

description: I am pleased to be working with a highly regarded regional law firm to appoint a Clinical Negligence Lawyer to join its well\-established claimant team.               This is an excellent opportunity to handle a varied, high\-quality caseload within a supportive and forward\-thinking environment, with the added benefit of a fully remote working model following an initial onboarding period where office attendance will be required in North Yorkshire for a couple of months.               The Role       You will manage a diverse caseload of claimant clinical negligence matters, advising and representing clients on complex and often life\-changing claims. The work will include:     * Catastrophic injury claims, including spinal cord and brain injuries * Cerebral palsy cases * Fatal accident claims * Claims against hospitals and medical professionals * Other high\-value and serious injury matters             You will be responsible for progressing matters from inception through to resolution, including:     * Reviewing and analysing medical records * Instructing medical experts and counsel * Taking witness statements * Negotiating settlements * Issuing and progressing court proceedings where required             The team is known for delivering exceptional client care to individuals often facing highly sensitive and distressing circumstances, so an empathetic and professional approach is essential.               Working Arrangements       This role offers fully remote working on a long\-term basis. However, to support onboarding, training and integration into the team, there will be an expectation to attend the North Yorkshire office for the first couple of months. Following this initial period, you will transition to a fully remote setup.               The successful candidate will:     * Have a solid Personal Injury background, ideally with 1–5 years’ PQE and experience in Clinical Negligence * Demonstrate a positive, proactive and commercially aware approach * Build strong, trusting relationships with clients * Deliver exceptional levels of client care, particularly when supporting vulnerable individuals * Ideally have some exposure to business development or marketing initiatives             You will be a team\-oriented individual who can quickly integrate into a collaborative environment and contribute to its ongoing success.               In addition to high\-quality work and a supportive culture, the firm offers:     * Competitive salary * Fully remote working (post\-onboarding period) * Clear progression framework with structured career development * Wellbeing support initiatives * Free on\-site parking (for office attendance periods) * Active social calendar * Comprehensive benefits package including pension, life cover, enhanced family leave, health cash plan, staff discounts, generous annual leave (including a birthday day off) and a cycle to work scheme             This is a fantastic opportunity for a Clinical Negligence Lawyer seeking high\-quality work, genuine flexibility and long\-term career progression within a respected regional practice.               For a confidential discussion or to apply, please get in touch.               If you are interested in hearing more, then apply directly via the link or contact Rachael Atherton at G2 Legal Recruitment.

description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.           In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.           For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.           In this three\-month summer internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth.          **A few examples of your responsibilities**  * **Investigate.**   Build a deep understanding of your product, its business model, and the drivers behind its growth. Use AI, data, and user research to quickly uncover meaningful opportunities and challenge assumptions. * **Innovate.**   Combine user insights, market signals, and emerging technologies to identify high\-impact initiatives and features. Use modern tools, first\-principles reasoning, and your creative insights to explore solution spaces quickly and sharpen product thinking. * **Execute.**   Turn ideas into impactful features. Prioritize ruthlessly, using data to focus on what truly moves the needle. Own the development process end\-to\-end, coordinating designers, engineers, and other teammates to build solutions that address users’ most important needs. * **Iterate.**   Use experimentation and product metrics to understand what works and what doesn’t. Treat every project as a learning loop, using insights from past work to improve the product continuously.   **What we look for**  * **Reasoning ability.**   Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.**   You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.**   You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.**   You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.   **What we offer**  * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.**   You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.**   We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.**   Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.**   Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.   **Commitment \& contract**  Internship. Full\-time or part\-time.          **Location**  Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.          **The selection process**  In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.           If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.           We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.           We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.           Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.           To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.           If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

We aim to bring discovery, inspiration, and joy back to shopping by making TikTok the commerce channel of choice for merchants, creators, and affiliates.With millions of loyal users globally, we believe TikTok is an ideal platform to deliver a brand new and better e\-commerce experience to our users. We are looking for passionate and talented people to join our product and operations team, to build an e\-commerce ecosystem that is innovative, secure and intuitive for our users and brands.           We are looking for talented individuals to join us for an internship in 2026\. Internships at TikTok aim to offer students industry exposure and hands\-on experience. Turn your ambitions into reality as your inspiration brings infinite opportunities at TikTok           Internships at TikTok aim to provide students with hands\-on experience in developing fundamental skills and exploring potential career paths. A vibrant blend of social events and enriching development workshops will be available for you to explore. Here, you will utilize your knowledge in real\-world scenarios while laying a strong foundation for personal and professional growth. It runs for 12 weeks.           Candidates can apply to a maximum of two positions and will be considered for jobs in the order you apply. The application limit is applicable to TikTok and its affiliates' jobs globally. Applications will be reviewed on a rolling basis. We encourage you to apply as early as possible. Please state your availability clearly in your resume (Start date, End date).          **Start Dates** * June 8th, 2026 * June 22nd, 2026 * July 6th, 2026    As one of key pillars of TikTok Shop growth in UK, LIVE ops team is responsible for building a strong pool of livestream merchants/creators through various key initiatives, establishing C\-end live mindset among users, and driving GMV growth through livestream commerce.          * Assist in conducting detailed data analysis for informed business decision * Assist in key central project facilitation and deeply engage in operational details to drive GMV impact (end\-to\-end delivery from live registration to post\-live review) * Assist in live playbook definition and development (integrating US/China good practices with UK characteristics) and help on dissemination of playbook through training organization (webinars, offline events, community management, etc.) * Assist in collaboration with cross\-functional teams (e.g., product, recommendation, GNE teams) for landing tactics and removing roadblocks to drive project success * Deliver ad\-hoc reports and analysis of livestream performance   **Qualifications**  Minimum Qualifications          * Current students in the penultimate year of their degree \& Major in Marketing, Business, Economics, Data Analytics, Communications, or related fields * Basic understanding and sense of TikTok Shop ecosystem, live ecosystem * Attention to detail, affinity with numbers, strong in problem\-solving    Preferred Qualifications          * Past experience in e\-commerce and live content * Experience with SQL, Tableau and other data analytics tools. * Ability to manage ambiguity and thrive in a fast\-paced working environment.    Job Information          **About TikTok**  TikTok is the leading destination for short\-form mobile video. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. TikTok's global headquarters are in Los Angeles and Singapore, and we also have offices in New York City, London, Dublin, Paris, Berlin, Dubai, Jakarta, Seoul, and Tokyo.           Why Join Us           Inspiring creativity is at the core of TikTok's mission. Our innovative product is built to help people authentically express themselves, discover and connect – and our global, diverse teams make that possible. Together, we create value for our communities, inspire creativity and bring joy \- a mission we work towards every day.           We strive to do great things with great people. We lead with curiosity, humility, and a desire to make impact in a rapidly growing tech company. Every challenge is an opportunity to learn and innovate as one team. We're resilient and embrace challenges as they come. By constantly iterating and fostering an "Always Day 1" mindset, we achieve meaningful breakthroughs for ourselves, our company, and our users. When we create and grow together, the possibilities are limitless. Join us.           Diversity \& Inclusion           TikTok is committed to creating an inclusive space where employees are valued for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. Our platform connects people from across the globe and so does our workplace. At TikTok, our mission is to inspire creativity and bring joy. To achieve that goal, we are committed to celebrating our diverse voices and to creating an environment that reflects the many communities we reach. We are passionate about this and hope you are too.

description: **Company Description** **About Revaia**  Revaia is a technology investor based in Paris, London, Berlin and Munich. We partner with the next generation of European digital scale\-ups and help them build leadership positions in a sustainable way.           We are a young, complementary, gender\-equal, and diverse team with entrepreneurial and finance backgrounds. We build bridges between venture, private and public markets.           We are sparring partners for innovative entrepreneurs who are working to transform our world for the better. So far, we have partnered with 20 technology companies: Synera (Industrial AI), Intersec (govtech), Definely (legal AI), Sekoia, (cybersecurity), Ampeco (charge point management solution), FASST (insurance distribution platform), Aircall (a cloud telephony software), GetAccept (a sales enablement software), gohenry (a mobile neobank for kids), Welcome to the Jungle (a digital workplace platform), Yubo (a social discovery mobile application), Frontify (a digital asset management platform), Hublo (a staff management solution for the healthcare sector), Planity (a marketplace for the beauty sector), Algolia (a search and discovery API solution), deepki (an ESG data intelligence platform), Coralogix (an in\-stream observability platform), platform.sh (a next\-generation hosting platform), Kaiko (a crypto market data platform), Mews (a cloud\-based hotel property management system) and Epsor (an employee savings and retirement solution).           We partner with and look for technology companies across a large spectrum of industries and business models. Our typical investment range is €15m to €30m, from series B to post\-IPO investments.          **Job Description**  Based in London, the intern will be part of the investment team and will be supervised by 9 investors in London, Paris, Berlin and Munich. She or he will be fully involved in all steps of the investment process.          **Key contributions include:** **Sourcing:**   in\-depth analysis of targeted sectors or geographies to identify potential new investment opportunities. Participation in industry forums and tech\-related events.          **Assistance in the investment process:**   market review, work with industry experts, financial analysis and modelling, participation in meetings with companies, advisors, and partners, preparation of investment memos for Investment Committee.          **Assistance in the fundraising process:**   preparation and update of presentation materials, sourcing of potential LPs, drafting of follow\-up emails.          **Preparation of weekly meetings:**   deal flow review, new investment opportunities, and presentation of analyses carried out.          **Portfolio support:**   work with and help support the entrepreneurs and teams of our portfolio companies.          **ESG support:**   make sure ESG processes and criteria are integrated all along the investment cycle and promoted among portfolio companies.          **Qualifications** **Profile we are looking for:** * Availability: Can you commit for 6 months starting July 2026? We're looking for full\-time dedication in this timeframe * Experience Requirement: At least one internship experience in finance (VC, PE, M\&A …) or strategy consulting * Mindset: We appreciate a strong entrepreneurial mindset and a keen interest in the technology ecosystem * Skills: Autonomy, multitasking, and full proficiency in English are essential * Work Eligibility: You must be eligible to work in the UK    Additional Information          **Start date:**   July 2026          **Location**   : London          **Duration:**   6 months          **Apply:**   Send the application through SmartRecruiters          ***Revaia will only consider candidates who have the legal right to work in the job’s location and will not provide sponsorship for this role.*** *At REVAIA we are committed to the work of advancing diversity, equity, and inclusion within our organization. We are committed to ensuring to all of our employees and potential candidates equal opportunities, without any consideration of gender, ethnic background, religion, sexual orientation, social status, physical abilities, or age.*

description: At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.           In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.           For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.           In this three\-month summer internship, you’ll contribute to priority projects, collaborate closely with skilled teammates, and build strong foundations for your growth.          **A few examples of your responsibilities**  * **Understand.**   Build deep knowledge of your product, its business model, and the key levers driving growth. * **Analyze.**   Use AI, automation, and advanced analytics to explore high\-performing campaigns, identify patterns in user and product data, and generate actionable insights. * **Ideate.**   Turn insights, experiment results, competitor research, and AI\-assisted exploration into hypotheses and identify opportunities to improve growth and monetization. * **Test.**   Design and run rigorous experiments with real users, partnering closely with designers, engineers, and analysts to bring ideas to life and iterate quickly based on results. * **Scale.**   Roll out winning ideas broadly, and use learnings from each experiment to shape the next cycle of discovery and testing.   **What we look for**  * **Reasoning ability.**   Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter. * **Drive.**   You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar. * **Team spirit.**   You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent. * **Proficiency in English.**   You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.   **What we offer**  * **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.**   You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field. * **An exceptional opportunity for growth.**   We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too. * **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.**   Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut. * **All. These. Benefits.**   Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.   **Commitment \& contract**  Internship. Full\-time or part\-time.          **Location**  Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.          **The selection process**  In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.           If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.           We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.           We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.          **Before you apply**  If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.           Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.           To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.           If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

description: The Summer Internship Program is for candidates in their penultimate year of a bachelor’s or master’s degree, graduating between September 2027 and August 2028\.           This program is designed to provide students with a challenging, meaningful and supportive internship experience that replicates, as closely as possible, the experience of being a full\-time BlackRock Analyst. The program begins with an orientation that offers a thorough overview of the firm and the opportunity to hear from a number of senior leaders.           Following orientation, interns receive on\-the\-job training and are given day\-to\-day responsibilities to contribute to their teams throughout the summer. Programming also features a speaker series, mentoring and various networking opportunities, including activities with our employee networks.           If you enjoy this program, you should explore our Full\-Time Analyst Program, which you can consider applying to after your internship.          **Important**  Candidates can apply for only one program (e.g., Summer Internship Program or Full\-Time Analyst Program) and up to two functions within that program (e.g., Portfolio Management and Investment Product Strategy \& Solutions). You must apply for both opportunities using the same program application.           If you withdraw your application, you cannot submit another application for this program this year.          **Next Steps**  Once you submit your application, you will receive an email to complete a pre\-interview assessment. You have up to five days to submit your pre\-interview assessment; if you fail to do so, your application will be automatically withdrawn. Exception: Candidates applying to work in our Shanghai office will NOT be invited to submit a pre\-interview assessment.          **Application Deadlines** **Hong Kong**   : Applications to be open to June 2026\.          **Singapore**   : Applications to be open to June 2026\.          **Tokyo**   : Applications are now open. As we recruit on a rolling basis, we encourage you to apply as soon as possible.

description: **About Hasbro**  Hasbro is a leading games, IP and toy company whose mission is to create joy and community through the magic of play. With 165 years of expertise, Hasbro delivers groundbreaking play experiences and reaches more than 1 billion fans annually around the world, through physical and digital games, video games, toys, licensed consumer products, location\-based entertainment, film, TV and more.           Through its franchise\-first approach, Hasbro unlocks value from both new and legacy IP, including MAGIC: THE GATHERING, DUNGEONS \& DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, HASBRO GAMES, NERF, TRANSFORMERS, PLAY\-DOH and PEPPA PIG, as well as premier partner brands. Powered by its portfolio of thousands of iconic marks and a diversified network of partners and subsidiary studios, Hasbro brings fans together wherever they are, from tabletop to screen.           For more than a decade, Hasbro has been consistently recognized for its corporate citizenship, including being named one of the 100 Best Corporate Citizens by 3BL Media, a 2025 JUST Capital Industry Leader, one of the 50 Most Community\-Minded Companies in the U.S. by the Civic 50, and a Brand that Matters by Fast Company. For more information, visit https://corporate.hasbro.com or @Hasbro on LinkedIn.           Summer 2026 Project Management Intern          **Hasbro is seeking enthusiastic, curious, and creative Project Management Interns to support brand teams such as NERF, My Little Pony, Hasbro Gaming, and Play\-Doh. Interns will help deliver low\-complexity projects end‑to‑end by supporting project planning, timelines, risk management, and cross‑functional coordination.** **The Internship Experience**    Hasbro’s Summer Internship Program offers a behind\-the\-scenes look at how a global play and entertainment company operates\- paired with the opportunity to make a real impact.           Interns are fully embedded on their teams and trusted to own meaningful business projects aligned to team priorities. Throughout the summer, you’ll contribute to work that matters while also being part of a broader global intern cohort, gaining exposure to professional development sessions, networking opportunities, and leadership engagement designed to support your growth and learning.          **What You Will Do As a Project Management Intern** * Support end‑to‑end product and project development, ensuring quality, cost, and schedule targets are met * Build and track project plans, timelines, milestones, and reports with cross‑functional teams * Facilitate team meetings, communicate action items, and provide regular program status update * Coordinate with global partners across Engineering, Design, Marketing, Packaging, Quality, and Legal * Identify and manage project risks, support issue resolution, and drive continuous improvement * Act as communication hub for all development \& delivery information with inside \& outside stakeholders * Maintain, and continuously improve milestone / Highlight / Management reports and ensure data is accurate in informational systems.   **What You’ll Bring** * Current undergraduate or graduate student (Spring 2026 graduates not eligible) * Available to work full‑time * At least one year of study in Project Management or a related field, this position is open to degree in all dicipline * Strong communication, organization, and time management skills * Intermediate skills in Excel, Word, Outlook, and web‑based tools * Previous project, co‑op, or product development experience preferred * Passion for collaboration, play, and consumer product development   **Program Details**   * Interview Timeline: February – April * Summer Internship Program Dates: * Start: Late June (target June 22\) * End: Late August (target August 21\) * Commitment: Full\-time (40 hours/week) * Location: Hong Kong   **Why Hasbro**    Hasbro internships are designed as a launchpad for early career talent. Interns are given meaningful responsibility, exposure to senior leaders, and opportunities to explore long\-term career paths within the company. Strong performance may lead to consideration for return or full\-time roles.

description: Join Macquarie Capital’s Critical Minerals and Energy advisory and capital solutions business in Asia, where you will benefit from our deep local institutional knowledge, global reach, and long‑term client relationships, and gain first‑class exposure to a broad range of financial transactions. We have a track record of developing and accelerating the careers of future industry leaders.           At Macquarie, our advantage is bringing together diverse people and empowering them to shape all kinds of possibilities. We are a global financial services group operating in 31 markets and with 56 years of unbroken profitability. You’ll be part of a friendly and supportive team where everyone \- no matter what role \- contributes ideas and drives outcomes.          **What role will you play?**  You will advise clients on acquisitions, mergers, business sales and capital raisings, as well as be responsible for creating, implementing and reviewing complex financial models, understanding key business drivers, and determining profitability. You will assist in coordinating transaction workstreams across mergers and acquisitions and capital raisings, and you will develop presentations, information memorandums, and investment committee/board papers for clients. Where applicable, you will play a key role in supporting the origination and execution of ECM transactions across Asia, including IPOs, follow‑on offerings, block trades, rights issues, and equity‑linked products.           In addition, you will collaborate closely with senior bankers and cross‑functional teams and frequently partner with colleagues in our team's key offices across Singapore, Australia, South Africa and Canada in a dynamic, fast‑paced environment that values precision, creativity, and teamwork.          **What You Offer** * 3\+ years’ of experience in an investment bank or boutique with M\&A or ECM advisory experience * Experience in the natural resources sector, or ideally in adjacent industries (infrastructure, industrials etc.) * Excellent financial modelling capabilities, gained through valuation, forecasting or scenario analysis, with the ability to build models from scratch e.g. DCF, LBO, merger models, etc * Strong understanding of financial statements * Strong communication skills, enabling you to present key insights to senior stakeholders * Team player approach and able to excel in a fast paced and challenging work environment * High energy level with the desire to contribute to the ongoing success and expansion of the Critical Minerals and Energy Group at Macquarie * Native fluency in Mandarin and professional‑level proficiency in English.    We love hearing from anyone inspired to build a better future with us, if you're excited about the role or working at Macquarie we encourage you to apply.          **What We Offer** **Benefits**  At Macquarie, you’re empowered to shape a career that’s rewarding in all the ways that matter most to you. Macquarie employees can access a wide range of benefits which, depending on eligibility criteria, include:          * 1 wellbeing leave day and 5 additional paid Flexi\-Leave days per year * 20 weeks’ paid parental leave for primary caregivers along with 12 days of paid transition leave upon return to work and 6 weeks’ paid leave for secondary caregivers * 2 days of paid volunteer leave and donation matching * Option to purchase additional annual leave * Benefits and initiatives to support your physical, mental, and financial wellbeing including comprehensive medical and life insurance cover and additional flexible wellbeing credits and access to the Rental Reimbursement Program * Access to a wide range of learning and development opportunities, including reimbursement for professional membership or subscription * Hybrid and flexible working arrangements, dependent on role * Reimbursement for work from home equipment   **About Macquarie Capital**  Macquarie Capital partners with businesses, startups and governments to connect ideas to capital and help drive innovation, growth and create real\-world impact. Our global corporate advisory, investing, development and equities capabilities are underpinned by a full spectrum of capital markets solutions and deep sector expertise, with a focus on infrastructure and real assets, technology, software and services, and resources, energy and materials.          **Our commitment to diversity, equity and inclusion**  We are committed to fostering a diverse, equitable and inclusive workplace. We encourage people from all backgrounds to apply and welcome all identities, including race, ethnicity, cultural identity, nationality, gender (including gender identity or expression), age, sexual orientation, marital or partnership status, parental, caregiving or family status, neurodiversity, religion or belief, disability, or socio\-economic background. We welcome further discussions on how you can feel included and belong at Macquarie as you progress through our recruitment process.           Our aim is to provide reasonable adjustments to individuals who may need support during the recruitment process and through working arrangements. If you require additional assistance, please let us know in the application process.

description: **Job Description** **YOUR IMPACT**  Are you passionate about research and generating investment ideas? We’re looking for a professional to join our Technology sector team in Hong Kong, to develop valuable insights and investment ideas for our clients.          **OUR IMPACT**  From macroeconomic forecasts to individual stock analysis, our team develops tools and insights to help shape investment strategies for clients and the firm. Our analysts work on client\-focused research in the equity, fixed\-income, currency, and commodities markets, mining big data that enters markets around the world each day to identify game\-changing insights. You’ll be part of a team that is intellectually curious, creative, analytical, and passionate about performing market research.          **How You Will Fulfill Your Potential** * Providing support to single stock research analysts * Building, maintaining and updating company forecast and valuation models * Analyzing market share, macro trend, and cross company and sub sector data analysis * Write research notes on companies under coverage * Working on company / sector\-specific projects * Conducting statistical analysis of economic data, fundamentals, and relevant correlations of the data * Update monthly reports and marketing presentations * Working closely with Content Management Group to ensure timely publication of research reports * Respond to ad hoc requests from clients, sales and traders   **Basic Qualifications** **SKILLS \& EXPERIENCE WE’RE LOOKING FOR** * University degree (finance, accounting major preferable) * More than 1 year of relevant work experience – preferably looking at the Technology subsector * Excellent verbal and written communication skills in English and Mandarin * Excellent analytical, organizational, and creative skills * Excellent skills in Microsoft Office applications (Excel, Word, PowerPoint) * Ability to work/conduct independently and with limited supervision * Meticulous attention to detail, strong analytical and organizational skills   **Preferred Qualifications** * Strong ability to perform financial statement, business, and valuation analysis * Comparable business/financial/consulting experience preferred * Demonstrated ability to think laterally and exercise good judgment * Self\-starter attitude with high level of enthusiasm for equity research. * Natural curiosity, critical thinking, logical thinking   **About Goldman Sachs**  At Goldman Sachs, we commit our people, capital and ideas to help our clients, shareholders and the communities we serve to grow. Founded in 1869, we are a leading global investment banking, securities and investment management firm. Headquartered in New York, we maintain offices around the world.           We believe who you are makes you better at what you do. We're committed to fostering and advancing diversity and inclusion in our own workplace and beyond by ensuring every individual within our firm has a number of opportunities to grow professionally and personally, from our training and development opportunities and firmwide networks to benefits, wellness and personal finance offerings and mindfulness programs. Learn more about our culture, benefits, and people at GS.com/careers.           We’re committed to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates with special needs or disabilities during our recruiting process.
